text.title: Launch Retro Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Launch Retro Template application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Retro MUST cover exactly 5 sections: Audience reaction, Messaging fit, Creative performance, Channel ROI, Lessons. Engineering retro shape is rejected.
rule.rationale: Marketing retros that copy engineering shape miss the message / creative / channel axes that drive launch outcome.

rule.statement: Each section MUST surface a before/after metric pair. Vibes-only retros are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without the metric pair the retro becomes opinion; metrics anchor the discussion to outcome.

rule.statement: Output MUST include explicit Keep / Change / Drop decisions per section. Open-ended notes are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without decisions the retro produces no behaviour change next launch.

rule.statement: Retro MUST commit exactly one named change for the next launch with a named owner. Multi-change commitments are rejected.
rule.rationale: One change is the cap solo operators can actually execute; multi-change lists become aspirational.

rule.statement: Session MUST cap at 90 minutes (60 facilitated + 30 write-up). Open-ended sessions are rejected.
rule.rationale: Past 90 minutes the conversation loses focus; the cap forces decision discipline.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
